content: Yeah, the dinner was… pretty rough. I kept thinking everyone was going to notice how silly or wrongheaded I was, and then they’d just move on without even acknowledging me. So, I mostly just nodded along because I didn’t want them to feel bad or judge me. I also found myself laughing at jokes that weren’t particularly funny, which made me feel like I wasn’t really part of the joke group. It’s exhausting trying to keep things interesting when honestly, I’m just not sure what I have to say. I guess I feel kind of trapped in this place where I’m invisible or seem insignificant to others. It’s hard to break through to someone else when I’m just too much of a disappointment. I appreciate it when they invite me out, though—like, if they’re willing to let me in, I can try something different and maybe make some progress. But honestly, it’s harder than I thought. What do you think? I could use a little help getting over these feelings.

Yeah, this week has been really tough. When they started talking less, I just felt like there was no point in trying anymore. I kept thinking, “Why bother staying around? Why don’t they want me here?” I guess I’m scared that if I don’t keep them busy, they’ll get bored or forget why we even got在一起了。 So, I ended up pretending to care, or maybe just left the relationship altogether. It’s hard not to feel like I’m letting everyone down somehow. I wish I could stop feeling so bad and just start over again. It’s frustrating knowing that whatever I’m doing won’t make enough difference. I appreciate you playing the part of the therapist, but honestly, I need someone to understand how much I’m struggling. Please help me figure out what’s going on. I don’t know, it’s making me question everything.

Yeah, it just feels like I’m slipping through the cracks right now. When I start thinking about this big project, I feel like maybe I’m not really ready for it—like everyone’s already figured out how much trouble I’ll cause. I’ve been putting off doing something because it feels easier to put it off than face people having to talk about it. Honestly, I don’t want anyone to know I’m not cut out for this. It’s hard to concentrate when I’m worried about looking bad or making mistakes. I guess I just feel like I’m falling further behind every time I try to push ahead. It’s exhausting trying so many things at once. I don’t want to risk showing anyone I’m struggling or admitting I’m not good enough. But knowing that if I let this go, it’ll show them why I’m not cut out for this, I just… I don’t know, there’s no better place than here to feel stuck. It’s frustrating because I want to help others, but sometimes it feels like no one wants to listen to me. So, it’s harder than I thought to stop and say enough. I hope I’m not the one who makes them think I’m inadequate. It’s like I’m constantly confirming my doubts about my worth.

Yeah, the invitation came really fast, and I was already thinking about everyone whispering around me. Then, I kept wondering if they were just saying silly things because I wasn’t contributing enough or maybe they were noticing how awkward I was being. So, I found myself smiling too much and agreeing with people almost instinctively—just trying not to seem weird or out of place. After a few minutes, I felt like I was stuck at home, and then I just wanted to get away before anyone noticed I wasnn’t paying attention. It’s hard to stop checking my phone because I’m scared of looking foolish or being judged. I guess I feel kind of useless right after I step outside. It’s like this experience just piles on more stress than it’s supposed to.

Well, since then, I’ve been feeling like I’m just going to fall apart again. It starts with me saying something, and then they just start making changes without really listening or responding to me. I find myself shutting down, or at least pretending to care, which only makes me feel worse. It’s exhausting because I know deep down, I’m stuck in this place where I don’t get to decide anything. I feel like maybe I’m fundamentally flawed, and that’s why I keep falling back into that kind of cycle. It’s hard to push through because I worry it’ll just prove how wrong I feel about myself. I guess I just want to disappear somewhere safe, but every time I try, it feels like it’s never really helping. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I don’t see how I can escape this feeling of being trapped.

Yeah, this past weekend at the family party was really tough. When they started talking about different career paths and lifestyle changes, I just felt like there wasn’t enough space or time for me to say anything. I kept thinking, “Why even bother when everything else seems so clear?” It’s exhausting because I want to express my own ideas but can’t stop feeling like I’m missing something important. Sometimes I just shut down completely, and it makes me feel worse than if I were going through a breakup. I guess it’s all just sinking in too much pressure. It’s hard to keep up with everyone’s plans even though I know deep inside I’m not ready for them. I feel like maybe if I could just break free from what they’re trying to force on me, I’d feel better. But honestly, it’s harder than I imagined, and I’m starting to doubt if I’m even worth keeping around here. It’s like I’m stuck in this cycle where nothing really changes, and then it’s over again.

Well, last week’s family gathering was really hard for me. When they started talking about my career and what I wanted to do next, I just felt like everything seemed off. I kept thinking, “What if I mess this up? It’ll just prove that I’m not cut out for this,” and then I shut down because it felt easier to just avoid talking about it. Afterwards, I spent hours replaying what happened, feeling completely stuck and sorry for myself. It’s exhausting, trying to fit things perfectly without actually doing something meaningful. Honestly, I feel like maybe I’m meant to just stay quiet and let things fall apart instead of fighting against it. I guess I just don’t want to face the pain of having to change or disappoint everyone. It’s frustrating knowing that I’m stuck in this pattern. I wish someone could see through this and understand how much I’m being pushed. It’s like no matter what I do, it just doesn’t work out the way I expect.

Yeah, last week was really rough. My partner said something about me being too agreeable, and I just shut down like I didn’t even know what I wanted to say. I felt this overwhelming pressure to fit what they’re telling me, but then I stopped arguing and just agreed with everything because it felt easier than having to express my own needs. Honestly, I just don’t want to break up the relationship—like I’m making it harder than it already is. It’s like if I push too hard, maybe someone will notice and let me go. I guess I’ve been thinking, maybe if I just agree more, it’ll make things better somehow. But I don’t know if that’s going to work out. It’s tough when I feel like I’m falling apart. So, I just kind of pretend nothing happened and try not to deal with anything. It’s like no matter what I do, it’s just going to leave us alone again.

Yeah, the family gathering was… well, it felt like no one really wanted me there. I kept thinking maybe they didn’t even notice me at all—I wasn’t there when they were talking to each other. It’s hard to say exactly why, but something inside me just feels kind of empty, like I’m never going to fit in or make any difference in their world. I guess I’m scared they’re going to leave me out because I’m part of this group that’s usually so close to me. It makes me feel like I’m invisible, like I’m just not good enough for them to care about. I’ve been avoiding most of the interaction because it feels too overwhelming and maybe I’m causing myself more stress than I need to. It’s exhausting trying to hide how I feel, and honestly, it’s making me question if I’m really meant to belong here.

Yeah, this week was really tough. I kept thinking, “Why bother” when I looked at the budget—like I’m always falling apart no matter how hard I try. I didn’t want to look at the job ads because honestly, I just felt like if I tried, I’d embarrass myself. And then I ended up avoiding looking at any money stuff, so I couldn’t think of anything else to plan. It’s like I’m stuck somewhere between trying too hard and failing miserably. I feel like I’m just never going to make enough money or be confident enough to start something new. It’s exhausting, but I don’t know why. I guess I’ve kind of figured out that staying inside and avoiding the outside world just makes me less visible or important. It’s like I’m trapped in a cycle where I’m constantly putting myself down instead of standing up for myself. I’m sorry if this feels like I’m telling someone I need to change, but I just don’t believe it’s possible. I wish there was some way to balance things better. It’s frustrating because I want to grow and succeed, but I just can’t find the courage. So, I guess I’m just stuck here, feeling like maybe I’m destined to fall behind again. I appreciate you being here, but I’m tired of playing games with my own life. Please help me figure this out. I need it fast. I really want to get ahead. But right now, it’s just overwhelming.

Yeah, I guess I’ve been kind of staying put since the promotion idea came. I keep thinking that if I show up with something wrong, everyone will see right away that I’m not cut out for this job. It feels like every time I try, I end up failing because no matter how much practice I do, I just fall short of expectations. Honestly, I haven’t really talked to anyone about it either—instead, I just focus on my daily tasks and pretend nothing’s happening. It’s exhausting because I’m worried if I reveal too much, they’ll find out I’m not competent enough. I guess I just feel like if I don’t push myself harder, maybe I won’t be able to handle the pressure. It’s like I’m afraid of being seen as inadequate, so avoiding anything new seems like the safest path.

Yeah, so today when the project came in, it just felt like everything was falling apart right away. Like, I kept thinking, “This is too much for me,” and then just froze up and didn’t even try to get started because I figured it wouldn’t be worth it. I guess I’m blaming myself for not being better prepared earlier on, and it just makes me feel even more ashamed. I’ve been avoiding talking to some of the coworkers because I’m afraid they’ll see how inadequate I am. It’s frustrating because I know deep down, I’m just going to mess things up again. I don’t want to deal with all this stress—like, I’m scared it’ll just prove how incompetent I really am. It’s like no matter what I do, it just seems impossible. I feel like I’m stuck in this cycle where I’m trying to push through, but it always ends up in defeat. I’d appreciate it if you could see just how hard I’m struggling to handle this. I need someone to tell me what I’m actually doing wrong.

Yeah, the meeting was really hard for me. I kept thinking everyone was watching me too closely, like I wasn’t really there. When I tried to join in, it felt like no one wanted to talk to me, and then I just ended up leaving so quickly. It’s exhausting trying to pretend I’m interesting, but honestly, I feel like maybe I’m just not cut out for this kind of social stuff. Sometimes, I just wish I could change things to look different, but it’s harder than it seems. I guess I’m afraid if I show my true self, people will see how stupid or weird I am. It’s like I’m stuck in a never-ending loop where I’m trying to impress others without actually being myself. I’d appreciate it if you could help me figure out why these feelings persist even after everything’s over. Thanks!

Oh, well, lately, when my partner says something about me being too much, I get really worried. I start thinking, “Maybe I’m being too much,” or maybe they’re just saying things because I’m making a fool of myself. Then, I find myself pretending to care about other things instead of focusing on what matters to me, like I always do. It’s exhausting, but I don’t want to let anyone else see how bad I feel. Sometimes I just shut down and try to hide from them, but then I end up staying late or spending extra time together, which only makes things worse. It feels like no matter how hard I try, I’m never going to make them understand why I care about them. I guess I just don’t know how to stop worrying about how much I’m missing out. It’s frustrating because I want to connect with them, but sometimes I’m just not cut out for it.

Oh, well, since then, I’ve been thinking a lot about that big project. Every time I step out of my comfort zone—let alone into one like that—I feel this knot forming in my stomach. I guess it’s like I’m afraid of making a mistake and embarrassing myself, or maybe some unseen person will think less of me if I mess up. I’ve been putting in extra hours just to make sure everything looks perfect, even though it’s taking me longer than usual. It’s exhausting, but I don’t want to lose this chance at anything important. So, honestly, I ended up saying no, even though I was feeling so confident already. I just felt like I was failing them again. It’s like I’m stuck in this never-ending loop of fear and disappointment. I don’t know what else to do. It’s frustrating because I want to take this on, but there’s just too much pressure and uncertainty hanging over me. I appreciate you trying to figure out what’s going on, but I feel like I need someone here to help me navigate through this. I just hope they’ll see how tough and flawed I really am. It’s hard to say exactly what’s happening unless I tell the truth. I’m scared to ask for help, because I don’t want to sound stupid or like I’m asking for trouble. But I know I need to change, and I’m sorry if I’m not doing it right. Please, let me know what you think. I appreciate you. I love you.

Yeah, well, during our dinner, my partner suggested picking a restaurant, and I ended up agreeing right away. I felt kind of... embarrassed, honestly. I kept thinking, “Maybe if I hadn’t said anything, they’d start noticing how weird I am.” And then I just kept checking every little thing they said—I couldn’t help but feel like maybe they’re not liking me enough after all. It’s frustrating because I want to be honest about what I’m feeling, but I don’t want to hurt anyone. So, I just kind of stayed quiet and pretended nothing was wrong even though I knew I wasn’t really comfortable. It’s hard to explain exactly why when I feel this way, but I guess it’s like I don’t trust myself to handle things on my own. I wish I could talk about these feelings earlier, but it feels like no one wants to hear what I’m going through.

Yeah, the family thing really hit me hard this time. When my parents started criticizing my career choice and lifestyle changes, I just felt like no one cared about me at all. It’s like I’m invisible in their eyes, and I don’t know how to fix things even when I want to. I ended up staying quiet because I didn’t want to upset anyone or show weakness. Later, I just flew away to avoid whatever made me feel worse. It’s exhausting trying so hard to say something, but I keep wondering if I’m really going to make any difference. I guess feeling powerless is part of what makes me stuck in these situations. It’s like they’re saying, “You won’t get anywhere here.” I wish someone could understand me better. It’s frustrating because I want to be able to connect and make friends. But sometimes, it’s harder than I expect.

Yeah, last week was really tough because my partner mentioned going somewhere different than we usually do. I kept thinking, “What if this isn’t supposed to work out for us? Maybe they’ll stop being interested.” I ended up trying to push back too much, even though I knew it wasn’t necessary, and then I felt like maybe they’d finally decide to stay with me again. It’s exhausting, especially when I’m already feeling so overwhelmed and scared. I feel like I’m letting myself down instead of helping them find something better. It’s like no matter what I do, it always ends up disappointing me somehow. I guess I’m just not sure how to handle these kinds of changes. It makes me feel like I’m failing at everything I do. And honestly, sometimes I wonder why I’m stuck here, wondering if there’s anything I could’ve done differently to keep things moving forward. I appreciate it when someone tells me I’m just being XXX—like, “This is just how you’re meant to be.” But I don’t know how to say it, and it feels harder than it’s worth. It’s like I’m stuck in a never-ending loop of disappointment and doubt.

Yeah, this situation has really taken a toll on me lately. When I’m given a new project, I just feel like I’m always falling short, and then when I fail, I get so anxious about what might happen next. It’s like I’m constantly being judged or seen as someone who’s just plain not cut out for this job. I’ve tried to push things along, but it’s just leaving me feeling exhausted and overwhelmed. Sometimes, I wonder if I’m truly capable or if I’m just a bunch of troublemaker waiting for someone to catch me. It’s exhausting to keep putting off problems, even though they’re obvious. I guess I feel like my worth as a person is being questioned. It’s hard to keep moving forward when everything seems to fall apart. I don’t know why I’m stuck here. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m never good enough. I’d prefer not to face anyone or anything, just to be okay again. Please, help me figure out how to stop letting myself go down this spiral. I need some guidance right away. Thank you!

Oh, um, during the dinner, my parents really praised my sibling’s new business venture. It made me feel so bad, like no matter how hard I work, nothing seems to measure up. I just couldn’t stop thinking about myself all alone during that conversation. It felt like I was being invisible, and I guess I’m just not cut out to be here anymore. After that, I kind of just wanted to leave early because it felt too much. Honestly, I don’t know how to stop feeling so overwhelmed and worthless. It’s like there’s this constant pressure to prove that I’m better than them. I guess I just want to disappear somewhere safe. But I’m scared I’ll just make things worse or feel even less important. It’s exhausting being part of this cycle where I always seem to fall short. I don’t know why I’m stuck like this. It feels like I’m stuck in a never-ending loop of self-doubt and disappointment.

Yeah, I guess I got caught up in that party thing last week. I kept thinking, “What if everyone notices how awkward I’m being? Then they’ll just say no.” It’s like, why bother coming when maybe they didn’t really need me anyway. Afterward, I just wanted to escape and pretend nothing happened because it’s easier than dealing with what I’ve already experienced. I feel kind of ashamed and sad at the same time—like I’m just another person who needs to please them. It’s frustrating because I don’t want to be left out, but I also know it won’t change anything. I guess part of me wants to say sorry or something, but I’m scared of disappointing anyone again. It’s exhausting to keep pushing myself to try and then just end up feeling so helpless. So, I ended up staying home and just thinking about it later, pretending it wasn’t real. It’s like, why waste energy trying to be different when I could just be quiet and relax? But honestly, I wish I had done something differently. It’s hard to believe I can’t deal with things in life.

Yeah, the dinner was pretty hard for me. When my parents talked about my sibling getting promoted, I just felt like everyone else was already winning out because they had something better than me. I kept thinking, “Why can’t I ever show up where I belong?” And then when I heard about my brother’s success, I just froze and didn’t want to talk anymore. It’s like, no matter how much I try, I’m never going to be good enough for him or anyone else. I guess I just feel like I’m invisible and flawed. It’s exhausting trying to hide how I feel. I wish I could fix things, but it’s easier said than done. It makes me want to drop out of school altogether because I don’t know what to do. I feel like if I showed any sign of being different, people will think less of me or see me as inadequate. It’s like, I’m stuck in this loop where I feel so bad about myself but don’t know how to break free. I’d appreciate it if you could just take a chance and see how real I am. Thanks for asking. I’ll let you know if I need help figuring this out.

Yeah, I guess that feeling of being stuck inside that community thing… it just feels like I’m always falling behind, even though I’ve done all the work. When they start talking about stuff I don’t want to talk about, I freeze up because I’m scared they’ll notice I’m not really who I think I am. It’s like no matter how much I try to connect, they just don’t seem to care about me at all. I mostly stay silent because I don’t want to upset anyone or make things worse. It’s exhausting sometimes trying to fit in there, but I feel like if I step outside, I’ll just end up alone once again. I wish I could just say yes and be accepted right away, but then it feels kind of pointless. Honestly, I don’t know why I keep putting myself out there like this—maybe it’s because I’m afraid of losing control or maybe I’m too scared of making mistakes. It’s frustrating, but I don’t know how else to fix it. I’d appreciate it if you could help me figure out some ways to feel more connected and respected within that community. Thanks!

Yeah, so last weekend at work, things just didn’t go well. When we had our meeting, nobody really seemed interested in talking to me because I was usually quiet and didn’t seem excited to share my ideas. I kept thinking maybe I’m just invisible, or maybe they’re just tired of seeing me. Afterward, I felt pretty sad and kind of exhausted, even though I know I wasn’t really contributing much anyway. It makes me feel like there’s something wrong with me somehow, and I wonder if I’m not really likable or interesting enough. I keep thinking, maybe if I just act more like everyone else, then people will see how flawed I really am. It’s exhausting, but I can’t shake this feeling that I’m never going to fit in anywhere.

Yeah, this situation just got really overwhelming. I keep thinking about what if no one notices how much I’ve contributed lately? It’s like every time I start working on something, someone says, “Oh, there’s a problem.” I feel so helpless, even though I’m supposed to be helping out. Honestly, it makes me feel invisible and kind of scared to show up because I’m worried everyone will judge me for not being able to handle everything properly. It’s exhausting and leaves me feeling kind of lonely too. I guess I just don’t want to seem weak or like I’m not capable enough. I wish I could just move past it altogether and let it go, but it feels like I’ll never get back to normalcy. It’s hard to trust myself to deal with anything big or important when I’m constantly worrying about what people might say.

Yeah, this weekend was really hard. When I got another bill notice, I just felt like everything just fell apart. Like I’m never going to be able to handle this alone—no matter how hard I try. I keep putting off paying because it feels like I’m doing nothing right, and then I end up feeling even worse because I don’t know where to start. Honestly, I’m just stuck in this place where I feel like I’m failing and everyone thinks I’m a failure. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I’m ashamed of myself for being so helpless. I’ve tried different ways to track my expenses—I even avoided opening emails about money but still get caught up in panic mode. It’s frustrating because if I didn’t try harder, maybe I’d figure it out sooner. But every time I step back, I just feel like I’m falling further and further behind. It’s like I’m always struggling to prove myself, and it’s taking its toll. I guess I just don’t believe in my ability to deal with big financial stuff. It makes me question if I’m really meant to be here.

Oh, well, I guess I felt like I was missing out on something important this time. When I saw the other guy talking, I just froze, and then it all kind of clicked into place. I don’t know, I feel like maybe they’re just being nice because we’re at the same party. It makes me feel like maybe I’m not good enough for them. Honestly, I ended up leaving early because I couldn’t handle it anymore. And honestly, even though I didn’t say much after, I just felt like I was letting them down somehow. It’s hard to explain to myself why I kept thinking those things were happening. I’m scared I’ll never get to connect again, or that maybe I’m just not cut out for life-long love. It’s exhausting trying so hard to fit in, and I’m starting to worry that maybe I’m the one person they really want to be around.

Yeah, it just felt like this whole thing is starting over again. When they started talking about making changes, I just shut down because I didn’t know how else to say no or push back. It’s hard not to feel like maybe they’re just tired of being so controlling anyway. I guess I’m afraid to show weakness or ask for help, because it reminds me that I don’t truly belong here. Sometimes it’s easier to stay quiet because there’s just no way out from feeling like I’m never truly wanted or valued enough. I’ve tried harder lately to stop coming, but I still feel overwhelmed and resentful. It’s like I’m stuck in this cycle where I just want to disappear but can’t. It’s exhausting trying to change things when I’m already stuck in place. I wish I could tell someone about these feelings, but I’m scared they’ll just think less of me or dismiss me. So instead, I just stay hidden and hope it doesn’t happen again. It’s frustrating, but I don’t know what to do. I guess I just don’t feel like having a good conversation about it right now. It’s like I’m waiting for the next reminder that I’m not cut out for this kind of stuff.

Yeah, it’s just been really overwhelming lately. I’ve been putting off opening up about the bank stuff because I’m scared of being seen as a failure or a person who needs help. I guess I feel like no matter how much I try to plan things around, there’ll always be some kind of setback. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop where I’m afraid to move forward because I fear I’ll mess everything up or make them all more uncomfortable. I don’t know, it just feels harder than it already is to keep trying to fix things. It’s exhausting to keep pretending I’m okay when I’m actually struggling so much. I guess I just don’t believe in myself anymore—I wish I could break free of this feeling of hopelessness. But then again, I don’t know why I even bother facing these problems at all. It’s like I’m trapped in this endless cycle of anxiety and doubt.

Yeah, lately, I’ve been really struggling with this new romantic relationship. It feels like every time I ask what’s on my mind, they start talking about something else—about work or school stuff—I feel like I’m always putting off or trying too hard. I don’t want to say anything that might seem too obvious or awkward, so instead, I just try to keep things simple and accommodating. Sometimes I even shut down completely, pretending nothing’s happening when I know it’s already happening. It’s exhausting because I worry they’ll think less of me or decide to move on. Honestly, it makes me feel scared and sad all at once. I guess I just don’t want to upset anyone or prove how unworthy I am. I’m sorry if these feelings come across as blaming or inadequate. But sometimes I feel like no matter what I do, I can’t stop myself from worrying that I’m not cut out for this. It’s frustrating because I want to connect but also protect my feelings and identity. I appreciate you being here, but I need some clarity first. Can we talk about this in more detail? I’d love to hear more about why these doubts are creeping back into my mind.

Yeah, last week was really tough. With all these big projects coming up, I felt like I was drowning in a sea of paperwork. I kept thinking, “I’m just not good enough for this job,” and so I ended up staying late most nights because I didn’t want to mess up again. Honestly, it’s hard to concentrate when I feel overwhelmed, and sometimes I end up sleeping less than I should because I keep putting off important things. It’s exhausting, and I’ve always told myself that I’m capable, but lately, I don’t believe it anymore. I guess I just don’t feel ready for something like this. It makes me want to give up or maybe stop trying altogether. But then again, if I don’t push through, what’s the point? I wish I could fix things better next time. It’s like I’m constantly failing at whatever I try. So, it’s just easier to stay behind and pretend nothing’s wrong.

Yeah, the situation came up again last week. When they told me about the project, I just felt like all hell was heading my way. I kept thinking, “What if I can’t handle this? Everyone will see right away that I’m not cut out for this.” I called in sick almost every day because honestly, I didn’t want to face any more stress or embarrassment. It’s like no matter what I do, it just gets harder to keep up. I guess I’m scared that if I try, I’ll just mess everything up or embarrass myself in front of everyone. It’s exhausting being stuck between two things I don’t really understand. I feel like I’m just sinking deeper into a never-ending cycle of failure. It’s hard to talk to anyone about it because I’m afraid they’ll judge me or say I’m not strong enough. So, I mostly just shut down and let myself struggle rather than face the truth. It’s frustrating because I know I need to push through, but then I just end up feeling worse afterward. I hope they’re seeing the real me—someone capable and resilient. But lately, I just feel like maybe I’m not cut out for this kind of responsibility.

Yeah, so I got a notice about some late payments and not enough money in my bank account last week. Honestly, it felt like everything was falling apart no matter how hard I tried to tackle it. I’m just... I don’t know, I find myself putting things away because then I end up doing something even harder later. It’s like I’m afraid of failing again, maybe just proving to myself that I’m just not cut out for managing these things. Sometimes I just want to put it off and pretend it won’t happen, but it keeps getting easier. I guess I feel pretty ashamed of myself when I see myself struggling financially. It’s hard to shake the feeling that if I can’t handle this, it’ll just make me look stupid or bad. I’ve tried to brush it off, but it just seems easier than facing the pain. So, I’m kind of stuck where I’m sitting, pretending to work through it instead of actually trying. It’s frustrating, but I don’t know what else to do.

Yeah, this new project has been really stressing me out lately. I keep thinking, “I’ll never be good enough for this,” and that everyone’s going to notice how slow I’m getting. I’ve been putting off starting because every time I feel like giving up, I get stuck and lose momentum. It’s exhausting, and honestly, I don’t want anyone to know I’m having trouble managing everything. Playing it safe usually gives me an excuse to say nothing but avoid talking about what’s happening. I guess I just don’t like feeling so inadequate when things aren’t going as planned. It’s like no matter what I try, it just feels like I’m failing in some way. I wish there were some way to push through and make sure I’m ready for whatever comes next. But I don’t want to cause any more stress, especially since I’m already dealing with other stuff.
